text: The thyroid hormones (THs), thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3) are products of the thyroid gland in all vertebrates. Their role in early development and metamorphosis is well established in mammals and amphibians, respectively, and recently several studies in fish have highlighted the importance of THs during metamorphosis including eyes (Power et al., 2001), Baumann et al., 2019, Darras et al., 2015, Bohnsack & Kahana, 2013)
